content:
# IVX 100M SCALE READINESS — STRESS TEST, CAPACITY AUDIT, FIX, DEPLOY AND VERIFIED EVIDENCE

MODE: EXECUTE NOW
PRIORITY: P0 / ENTERPRISE SCALE
GOAL: Determine the real tested capacity of IVX and build a verified path toward supporting 100 million registered users.

IMPORTANT:
- Do not claim IVX supports 100M users without measured evidence.
- Do not run uncontrolled destructive traffic against production.
- Use a production-like staging environment first.
- Protect real users, real investor data, real payments and real Supabase records.
- Do not expose secrets.
- Do not ask for credentials unless a real runtime test proves missing, expired, revoked, 401 or 403.
- Return measurements, commands, logs and deployment proof—not narrative.

==================================================
DEFINITION OF THE 100M TARGET
==================================================

Separate these concepts:

1. REGISTERED USER CAPACITY
   Target: database and identity architecture capable of holding 100,000,000 user accounts.

2. MONTHLY ACTIVE USERS
   Measure projected load at:
   - 100,000 MAU
   - 1,000,000 MAU
   - 10,000,000 MAU
   - 100,000,000 MAU

3. CONCURRENT USERS
   Test progressively:
   - 10
   - 100
   - 1,000
   - 5,000
   - 10,000
   - 25,000
   - 50,000
   - 100,000 concurrent virtual users

4. REQUEST THROUGHPUT
   Measure sustained and burst traffic:
   - 100 requests/second
   - 1,000 requests/second
   - 5,000 requests/second
   - 10,000 requests/second
   - 25,000 requests/second
   - 50,000 requests/second

Do not interpret “100M users” as 100M simultaneous live connections.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — DISCOVER THE REAL ARCHITECTURE
==================================================

Audit and document:

- Native Expo app
- Landing page
- Owner AI chat
- Public API
- Private API
- Render services
- Supabase Auth
- Supabase database
- Supabase Realtime
- Supabase Storage
- Redis/cache, if present
- background workers
- queues
- cron jobs
- websocket/socket transport
- notifications
- media delivery
- CDN
- DNS
- GitHub deployment pipeline
- payment/investment services
- analytics
- logging and monitoring

For every component record:

- service name
- environment
- repository
- branch
- deployed SHA
- instance size
- replica count
- autoscaling configuration
- CPU limit
- memory limit
- connection limit
- timeout
- rate limit
- database pool size
- storage limits
- single point of failure
- estimated maximum capacity

==================================================
PHASE 2 — IDENTIFY EVERY HIGH-TRAFFIC ROOT
==================================================

Discover and test all real user-critical roots, including:

AUTH:
- register
- email verification
- phone verification
- login
- refresh token
- logout
- password recovery
- owner login

FEEDS:
- main feed
- reels feed
- landing feed
- pagination
- media metadata
- likes
- comments
- saves
- shares

DEALS:
- property listing
- deal detail
- View Deal
- Invest Now
- search
- filters
- saved deals
- portfolio

CHAT:
- create conversation
- send message
- load history
- realtime receive
- attachment upload
- image delivery
- reconnect
- message persistence
- Owner AI request

MEMBERS/INVESTORS:
- member list
- investor list
- profile
- search
- role lookup

MEDIA:
- image upload
- video upload
- thumbnails
- streaming
- signed URLs
- storage access

For each discovered route return:

- route
- HTTP method
- auth type
- database calls
- external provider calls
- cache behavior
- average payload
- current latency
- current error rate
- rate limit
- PASS/FAIL

==================================================
PHASE 3 — BUILD A SAFE LOAD-TEST ENVIRONMENT
==================================================

Create or verify a staging environment that mirrors production:

- same application code
- same runtime versions
- equivalent database schema
- equivalent indexes
- equivalent service topology
- production-like autoscaling
- synthetic users only
- synthetic deals
- synthetic conversations
- synthetic media metadata
- no real payments
- no real investor transactions
- no email/SMS spam
- isolated test storage bucket
- isolated test database/project when possible

Create synthetic datasets:

- 100,000 users
- 1,000,000 users
- 10,000,000 user capacity model
- 1,000,000 properties/deals model where feasible
- 10,000,000 feed interactions model
- realistic conversations and messages
- realistic media references

If generating the full dataset is too expensive, use database-size extrapolation with measured row size, index size and query plans. Clearly label extrapolated results versus actual executed tests.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — OBSERVABILITY BEFORE LOAD
==================================================

Before testing, enable measurable monitoring for:

- CPU
- memory
- event-loop delay
- process restarts
- request count
- requests per second
- p50 latency
- p90 latency
- p95 latency
- p99 latency
- maximum latency
- HTTP 4xx rate
- HTTP 5xx rate
- timeout rate
- retry rate
- database connections
- connection pool saturation
- slow queries
- lock waits
- deadlocks
- index hit rate
- cache hit rate
- queue depth
- job delay
- websocket count
- realtime disconnects
- storage upload latency
- AI provider latency
- external API failures
- cost per test stage

Every load-test run must have:
- run ID
- timestamp
- git SHA
- deploy ID
- environment
- dataset size
- test script version

==================================================
PHASE 5 — BASELINE TESTS
==================================================

Run baseline tests first:

1. Single-user functional test
2. 10 concurrent users
3. 100 concurrent users
4. 1,000 concurrent users

Workload mix:

- 25% feed reads
- 15% deal detail reads
- 10% search
- 10% authentication/session refresh
- 10% likes/saves/comments
- 10% member/investor reads
- 10% chat message operations
- 5% attachment metadata
- 5% Owner AI or AI-disabled representative task

Report:

- total requests
- successful requests
- failed requests
- p50/p95/p99
- requests/second
- CPU
- memory
- DB connections
- errors by route

Fix all baseline failures before continuing.

==================================================
PHASE 6 — PROGRESSIVE LOAD TEST
==================================================

Run progressive stages. Stop automatically if safety thresholds are exceeded.

STAGE A:
- 1,000 concurrent users
- 15 minutes

STAGE B:
- 5,000 concurrent users
- 20 minutes

STAGE C:
- 10,000 concurrent users
- 30 minutes

STAGE D:
- 25,000 concurrent users
- 30 minutes

STAGE E:
- 50,000 concurrent users
- 30 minutes

STAGE F:
- 100,000 concurrent users
- only after prior stages pass
- 30 minutes

Safety stop thresholds:

- error rate above 1%
- p95 above 2 seconds for normal reads
- p99 above 5 seconds
- CPU above 85% for 5 minutes
- memory above 85%
- database pool above 85%
- sustained queue growth
- repeated service restart
- data corruption
- duplicate financial action
- authentication instability
- realtime disconnect rate above defined threshold

Record the exact first breaking point.

==================================================
PHASE 7 — SPIKE, SOAK AND FAILOVER TESTS
==================================================

SPIKE TEST:
- increase traffic 10x within 60 seconds
- verify autoscaling
- verify no cascade failure
- verify rate limits
- verify recovery

SOAK TEST:
- run the maximum stable load for 8 hours
- check memory leaks
- connection leaks
- queue growth
- database bloat
- log growth
- storage errors
- degraded latency

FAILOVER TEST:
- restart one backend instance
- remove one instance
- restart worker
- simulate database connection interruption
- simulate Redis/cache loss
- simulate AI provider timeout
- simulate storage timeout
- confirm graceful recovery

DEPLOYMENT TEST:
- deploy during sustained test traffic
- verify zero or controlled downtime
- verify session continuity
- verify no message loss
- verify no duplicate requests

==================================================
PHASE 8 — DATABASE SCALE AUDIT
==================================================

Audit every high-volume table:

- users/profiles
- members
- investors
- properties
- investments
- deals
- reels
- likes
- comments
- saves
- conversations
- messages
- notifications
- transactions
- audit logs
- media

For each table:

- current row count
- projected 100M-scale row count
- average row size
- total projected storage
- index size
- primary key strategy
- partition strategy
- retention policy
- archive policy
- slowest query
- query plan
- missing indexes
- duplicate indexes
- RLS cost
- pagination method
- offset pagination risks
- cursor pagination readiness

Required fixes:

- cursor-based pagination for large feeds
- indexes for all frequent filters/joins
- no unbounded SELECT
- no N+1 query loops
- no full-table count on hot paths
- partition high-volume event/message tables when required
- connection pooling
- read replicas or equivalent plan
- archival strategy
- backup and restore test

==================================================
PHASE 9 — REALTIME AND CHAT SCALE
==================================================

Test:

- 1,000 websocket/realtime connections
- 5,000
- 10,000
- 25,000
- maximum supported by environment

Measure:

- successful connections
- reconnect rate
- dropped messages
- duplicate messages
- ordering failures
- message delivery p95
- persistence latency
- history load latency
- fan-out behavior
- server memory per connection

Acceptance:

- zero lost persisted messages
- zero duplicated message IDs
- deterministic ordering
- automatic reconnect
- no infinite reconnect loop
- no process crash

==================================================
PHASE 10 — MEDIA SCALE
==================================================

Test:

- image feed traffic
- video poster traffic
- signed URL generation
- upload initiation
- upload completion
- CDN caching
- cache misses
- slow network
- concurrent uploads

Do not run massive full-video uploads unnecessarily. Use controlled representative media sizes.

Report:

- storage throughput
- origin bandwidth
- CDN hit rate
- signed URL latency
- upload error rate
- estimated bandwidth cost at:
  - 100K MAU
  - 1M MAU
  - 10M MAU
  - 100M MAU

==================================================
PHASE 11 — SECURITY AND ABUSE UNDER LOAD
==================================================

Verify:

- rate limiting
- IP throttling
- account throttling
- bot protection
- login brute-force protection
- OTP abuse prevention
- upload abuse limits
- malformed payload handling
- oversized payload rejection
- unauthorized route protection
- owner-route protection
- no RLS bypass
- no secret leakage
- no log injection
- no duplicate investment/payment actions

Run controlled abuse tests only in staging.

==================================================
PHASE 12 — FIX THE BOTTLENECKS
==================================================

For every failed stage:

1. Identify the exact bottleneck.
2. Capture evidence.
3. Implement the smallest safe fix.
4. Add regression coverage.
5. Deploy to staging.
6. Rerun the same stage.
7. Compare before and after.
8. Continue only after the stage passes.

Possible fixes include:

- add indexes
- remove N+1 queries
- introduce caching
- add Redis
- add job queues
- split synchronous work
- add horizontal replicas
- tune connection pooling
- batch writes
- reduce payload size
- use cursor pagination
- move media to CDN
- use read replicas
- isolate AI workloads
- enforce rate limits
- partition large tables

Do not claim improvements without before/after metrics.

==================================================
PHASE 13 — 100M CAPACITY MODEL
==================================================

After real testing, create a capacity model for 100M registered users.

Calculate:

- total user/profile storage
- authentication record storage
- expected daily active users
- expected peak concurrent users
- requests per active user
- peak requests/second
- feed reads
- deal reads
- chat messages
- realtime connections
- media bandwidth
- database writes
- log volume
- backup size
- restore time
- monthly infrastructure cost
- AI cost
- storage cost
- bandwidth cost
- monitoring cost

Show assumptions clearly.

Return three architecture tiers:

TIER 1:
- up to 100K active users

TIER 2:
- up to 1M active users

TIER 3:
- 10M–100M registered-user architecture

For each tier list:
- infrastructure
- replicas
- database topology
- caching
- queues
- storage/CDN
- monitoring
- estimated monthly cost
- known risks

==================================================
PHASE 14 — PRODUCTION CANARY
==================================================

Only after staging tests pass:

1. Deploy safe performance fixes to production.
2. Confirm GitHub SHA.
3. Confirm deploy ID.
4. Run a low-risk production canary.
5. Use synthetic or internal accounts.
6. Limit traffic.
7. Monitor errors.
8. Roll back automatically if thresholds fail.

Do not attempt a 100K-user load test directly against live production without written owner approval and safeguards.

==================================================
MANDATORY FINAL REPORT
==================================================

TASK UNDERSTOOD:
[exact scope]

TEST ENVIRONMENT:
- Environment:
- Git SHA:
- Deploy ID:
- Services:
- Dataset:
- Load tool:
- Test script version:

CURRENT ARCHITECTURE:
- Backend:
- Database:
- Auth:
- Realtime:
- Cache:
- Queue:
- Storage:
- CDN:
- Monitoring:

ROUTES TESTED:
1. Route:
   Method:
   Load:
   p50:
   p95:
   p99:
   Error rate:
   Result:

LOAD-STAGE RESULTS:
- 10 users:
- 100 users:
- 1,000 users:
- 5,000 users:
- 10,000 users:
- 25,000 users:
- 50,000 users:
- 100,000 users:

MAXIMUM VERIFIED CAPACITY:
- Concurrent users:
- Requests/second:
- Test duration:
- p95:
- p99:
- Error rate:
- CPU:
- Memory:
- DB connections:

BREAKING POINT:
- Stage:
- Root cause:
- Evidence:

DATABASE RESULT:
- Largest tables:
- Slow queries:
- Indexes added:
- Pool saturation:
- Projected 100M storage:

REALTIME RESULT:
- Maximum connections:
- Delivery p95:
- Message loss:
- Duplicates:
- Reconnect result:

SPIKE TEST:
- PASS/FAIL
- Evidence:

SOAK TEST:
- PASS/FAIL
- Duration:
- Memory leak result:

FAILOVER TEST:
- PASS/FAIL
- Recovery time:

SECURITY LOAD TEST:

- Rate limits:
- Abuse controls:

FIXES IMPLEMENTED:
1.
2.
3.

BEFORE/AFTER:
- Metric:
- Before:
- After:

100M CAPACITY MODEL:
- Registered users:
- Projected peak concurrent users:
- Projected RPS:
- Storage:
- Bandwidth:
- Monthly cost:
- Required architecture changes:

GITHUB PROOF:
- Repository:
- Branch:
- Commit SHA:
- Push timestamp:

DEPLOYMENT PROOF:
- Service:
- Deploy ID:
- Deployed SHA:

REMAINING RISKS:
- exact risks or NONE

FINAL STATUS:
- VERIFIED CURRENT CAPACITY: [number]
- 100M REGISTERED-USER READINESS: VERIFIED / PARTIAL / NOT VERIFIED

RULE:
Do not state “100M verified” unless the final report contains real test commands, run IDs, metrics, logs, infrastructure configuration and reproducible evidence.
